title: FIGS Beats Q1 2026 Forecasts but Shares Drop 19.79 Percent
text: FIGS exceeded expectations with EPS of $0.03 (forecast $0.01) and revenues $159.9 million (forecast $152.48 million). Achieved 28% YoY revenue growth and expanded adjusted EBITDA margin to 8.7%. Despite strong results, stock fell 19.79% in after-hours trading to $11.63, recovering slightly to $11.78 premarket. Decline driven by multiple factors: Profit-taking after strong 136% run-up over past year. Rising margin pressures including tariff pressure, higher inbound freight from oil price surges, higher marketing expenses impacting profitability. Forward guidance caution with management embedding prudent warnings given pressures and uncertainties consumers face. Valuation concerns at P/E ratio 52.81 creating vulnerability to profit-taking despite strong growth. Disconnect between earnings performance and stock reaction reflects investor concerns about sustainability amid mounting cost headwinds.
